slicer 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
    • (Sliced) Wasserstein - Gaussian process regression workflow

Gaussian radial basis function kernel

Source: R/kernels.R
rbf.Rd

Builds a Gaussian radial basis function kernel matrix from a distance matrix.

Usage

rbf(D, length_scale = 1, variance = 1, squared = TRUE)

Arguments

D

A matrix with pairwise (possibly squared) distances.

length_scale

The length-scale parameter.

variance

The kernel variance parameter.

squared

If TRUE (default), D is assumed to contain squared distances. Set to FALSE if this is not the case.

Value

A kernel matrix with the same dimensions as D.

Examples

M <- rbind(c(0, 2, 1),
           c(2, 0, 2.5),
           c(1, 2.5, 0))
rbf(M, squared = FALSE)
#>           [,1]       [,2]       [,3]
#> [1,] 1.0000000 0.13533528 0.60653066
#> [2,] 0.1353353 1.00000000 0.04393693
#> [3,] 0.6065307 0.04393693 1.00000000
